name: California Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: California Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Title Protection Only (Business and Professions Code Section 2585)

address.description:
California State Board of Pharmacy
1625 North Market Blvd, N219
Sacramento, CA 95834

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a California pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The California State Board of Pharmacy fee schedule lists a $120 pharmacy technician application fee effective January 1, 2025. The biennial renewal fee is $150.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does California have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to California?

acceptedAnswer.text: The board says to allow 45 days to process a pharmacy technician application before checking status.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the California Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in California.
